<!-- Generated by the passage-export-control-verification workflow (36 agents). This is an AI-research VERIFICATION doc: the adversarial layer caught several fabricated citations — treat as a primary-source-anchored LEAD LIST, NOT ground truth. Every legal-VALUE change must be re-verified at primary source + (for interpretive items) lawyer-signed before commit. -->

# MASTER Export-Control Verification Report — Caelex Passage (Trade Surface)

**Compiled:** 2026-06-05 · **Sources:** 10 domain agents + adversarial challenge layer · **Code under audit:** `fix/passage-screening` / `fix-atlas-correctness` worktrees · **Scope:** EAR/ITAR/MTCR/AWV/EU-dual-use country groups, de-minimis, FDPR, parametric thresholds, citations, sanctions sources, classifier wiring.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

**Overall state.** The Trade subsystem's _structural_ compliance spine is largely sound — the FDPR destination-scope gates (`§734.9(b)/(c)/(d)/(f)`), the 25%/10% de-minimis selection in the authoritative `cascade.ts`, the OFAC 50%-cascade math, and 5 of 8 parametric thresholds all match primary regulation verbatim. But the audit surfaced **multiple CRITICAL defects that either let a controlled export through or surface fabricated legal text to operators**, plus three large engineering gaps (a dead control-list corpus, a 13-entity sanctions seed, and no snapshot-staleness gate).

**Verdict tally (39 distinct items):**

| Status                                                                                           | Count  |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------ |
| CONFIRMED corrections to apply (WRONG/STALE/MISSING, HIGH/MED conf, challenge CONFIRMED or none) | **26** |
| Verified correct — MATCH, no change                                                              | **17** |
| NEEDS HUMAN/LAWYER (REFUTED challenge, UNVERIFIABLE, or LOW confidence)                          | **3**  |
| Engineering P0 work-items (design/wiring)                                                        | **4**  |

(Some items are both a finding and a verified-clean control; counts reflect the dominant disposition. Two CONFIRMED items carry citation caveats requiring a re-cite before commit — flagged inline.)

**Top CRITICAL items (apply first, with the caveats noted):**

1. **CG-UA-D1** — Ukraine hardcoded in `D:1` (→`D:3`/`D:4`). UA was moved to Group **B** in 2020 (85 FR 84211). Mis-fires NS-FDPR/CB/MT against an EAR-favorable destination. _(Direction: over-restriction, but a true misclassification.)_
2. **DM-3 / DM-1 (de-minimis-reconcile)** — Fabricated "10% = Country Group D:1" de-minimis rule in `bom-de-minimis/calculator.ts`; three drifting EAR destination stores. No D:1-specific 10% threshold exists.
3. **TH-1** — USML XV(a)(7)(i) aperture operator **inverted** (`lt 0.5` vs reg's `> 0.50 m`). A >0.5 m ITAR imaging satellite is mis-screened as sub-threshold. **Genuine leak.**
4. **TH-9** — ECCN **7A005** controlled on a non-existent "velocity ≥600 m/s / altitude >18 km" criterion; the 600 m/s test belongs to **7A105**, and 7A005's real triggers (decryption ranging-code, adaptive-antenna) are missed. **Genuine leak (both directions).**
5. **AWV-3** — Entire `§9(2) AWV` 19-country "arms-embargo military catch-all" is **fabricated**; §9(2) is the nuclear notification duty. Also a true _gap_: real §9(1) nuclear coverage of IL/JO/PK/DZ is missing.
6. **CITE-3** — OFAC citation "JY-2026-013 / January 2026" is **fabricated** (no such press-release number; real doc is a Sanctions Advisory dated **March 31 2026**). Hard-coded across ~5 files.
7. **SRC-UK-1** — UK OFSI `ConList.csv` URL is **dead (404)**; the list closed 28 Jan 2026. UK screening currently matches **nobody**. Repoint to the FCDO UK Sanctions List.
8. **SRC-ANNEXIV-1** — EU Reg 833/2014 **Annex IV** is a 13-entity hand seed vs a real list of **500–700+**; ~98% of listed military end-users screen CLEAN. **Genuine leak** on a hard-prohibition list.
9. **DCW-1** — Full control-list corpora (Wassenaar ML\*, USML XV(g)-(i), DE Ausfuhrliste, India SCOMET, Japan METI) have **zero classifier consumers**; a declared ITAR/military code fires nothing. **Genuine leak.**
10. **SNAPSHOT-STALENESS-3** — No TTL gate on sanctions snapshots; a party can be CLEAR against a months-old OFAC list. **Genuine leak.**

---

## 3. NEEDS HUMAN / LAWYER Confirmation — DO NOT AUTO-APPLY

These items are excluded from the confirmed table because they were **REFUTED**, are **UNVERIFIABLE**, or carry **LOW confidence**. They must be reviewed by a human (export-control lawyer for the regulatory ones) before any change.

| ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **CG-D1-INCOMPLETE**                      | D:1 (NS) membership — proposed 24-country list                           | **Challenge verdict = REFUTED.** The agent's "D:1 = 24 countries" and its verbatim quote are **factually wrong** — live eCFR shows D:1 has **48 countries** (every Group-D country is X-marked in D:1). Following the agent's proposedFix (add only 8, remove UA) would still leave D:1 missing 24 codified members **including Iran and Syria**, which gate NS-FDPR — itself a CRITICAL under-coverage. The agent also wrongly claimed "Moldova is D:1-only" (eCFR: Moldova = D:1 AND D:2). | Do **NOT** apply the agent's list/quote. Re-populate `COUNTRY_GROUP_D1` from the authoritative **48-country eCFR D:1 column** (`ecfr.gov/api/versioner/v1/full/2026-06-03/title-15.xml?…part=740`), noting many overlap E:1/E:2/D:5. **Remove UA** (the one confirmed-correct sub-claim). Lawyer to sign off the full 48-member set. *(Note: CG-UA-D1's UA removal is independently CONFIRMED and may proceed; the D:1 *repopulation* is the blocked part.)* |
| **FDPR-7** (D:1 snapshot for NS-FDP gate) | `country-groups.ts:79-105` self-described "conservative snapshot" of D:1 | **Discrepancy = UNVERIFIABLE, confidence = LOW.** Agent could not line-verify every member against the live Supplement; an **OMISSION here could let a controlled FDP export through** (false-negative).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Reconcile `COUNTRY_GROUP_D1` against the live Supplement No.1 [NS column] (the 48-country list from CG-D1-INCOMPLETE). Add a dated provenance comment + a snapshot test. **This is the same underlying D:1 list as CG-D1-INCOMPLETE — resolve them together under lawyer review.** Prioritize: D:1 is load-bearing for two FDP gates.                                                                                                                        |
| **AWV-6**                                 | `WMD_CONCERN_COUNTRIES` (incl. IL, IN) presented as Art.4 trigger        | **Discrepancy = UNVERIFIABLE, confidence = LOW, no primary source exists** ("BAFA Länder mit Proliferationsbezug" is not published as a fixed statutory list). EU Art.4 is end-use based with no country list.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Keep as a conservative risk overlay but **stop labelling destination-only hits as "Art.4 EU 2021/821"**; relabel "WMD/proliferation-concern destination (heuristic)". Add a comment that membership (esp. IL/IN) is a curated judgement, not citable. A human/lawyer decides the list contents; do not present as a legal determination.                                                                                                                     |

## 5. Engineering Work-Items (P0)

### P0-A — Dead control-list corpus wiring (DCW-1) · **CRITICAL · challenge CONFIRMED**

**Problem.** Only two narrow classifier paths are live: the parametric matcher (binds to the ~99-entry `CONTROL_LIST_CROSS_WALK` seed) and the topic/property path (traverses `ALL_CLASSIFICATION_ENTRIES` = only 5 corpora). The full corpora — **Wassenaar Cat 6-9 (~70), Japan METI (~40), India SCOMET (~32), DE Ausfuhrliste (incl. 0010j + national catch-alls), USML XV(g)-(i)** — have **zero classifier consumers**. A declared ITAR USML XV(g) code or a Wassenaar ML code fires **nothing** → controlled item passes silently. No code/keyword classification path exists for item-create (`searchEntries`/`findEntry` are used only by the Astra chat tool).

**Recommended approach — Option C (hybrid), zero external cost, TDD:**

1. **Data-model bridge** — new `comply-v2/trade/classification/corpus-normalizer.ts` defining `NormalizedCorpusEntry` + one pure adapter per shape (3 shapes: ClassificationEntry-like; `de-ausfuhrliste` position-keyed; `usml-xv` paragraph-keyed). Export `NORMALIZED_FULL_CORPUS` (~250-300 static entries).
2. **Barrel fix** — `src/data/trade/index.ts`: re-export the orphan `*_ENTRIES`/`*_ENUMERATION` consts; fix the stale `ALL_ENTRIES` doc example (it's `ALL_CLASSIFICATION_ENTRIES`).
3. **Code/keyword path** — new pure `corpus-search.ts` `matchByCodeOrKeyword()` (declared-code exact / code-substring / keyword), attaching the existing `MATCHER_DISCLAIMER`.
4. **Union at entry points** — `classify-suggest.ts`, `classification-draft-builder.ts`, `item-parametric-classification.ts`: call `matchByCodeOrKeyword` alongside `matchAgainstCrossWalk`, merge (parametric first, dedupe by code, corpus-only as additional candidates with a distinct UI bucket). Make `property-trigger-engine.ts`/`classify-item.ts` raise the ITAR/military flag on a declared-code/keyword hit so the leak closes on the **determination** side, not just suggestions.
5. **Keep the parametric matcher authoritative** for predicates (predicate-less entries are refuted at `parametric-matcher.ts:~471` — do NOT route flat entries through it).
6. **Tests (write first):** per-corpus coverage; declared-code (`ML10.a`, `XV(g)(1)`, `0010j`); keyword; **leak-proof regression (declared USML XV(g) → ITAR flag through merged classify-item)**; union/dedup; disclaimer+safety; barrel-coverage.

**Immaterial caveats (do not block):** `usml-xv-a` exists only as a test fixture (no `usml-xv-a.ts`); entry counts are approximate (wassenaar ~70 vs claimed 71, etc.).

### P0-B — De-minimis single-source-of-truth + AWV rename (DM-1 reconcile + DM-3) · **CRITICAL · challenge CONFIRMED**

**Problem.** Three drifting EAR destination stores (`country-groups.ts` authoritative; `de-minimis-calculator.ts` duplicate with no 10% path + flat E-block; `classify-item.ts:63` third copy). The `bom-de-minimis` calculator invents a D:1→10% rule (DM-3). `art9` is a misnomer (set in the §8-AWV block).

**Recommended approach (TDD, on a Trade branch):**

- Single source = `country-groups.ts`. Delete duplicate `EMBARGOED`/`RESTRICTED` literals and `classify-item.ts:63` `RESTRICTED_DESTINATIONS`. Add `isComprehensiveEmbargoDest` + `deMinimisTierForDestination` (→ `REDUCED_10` for E:1/E:2 else `STANDARD_25`); keep 0% (a) carve-outs in `no-de-minimis-carve-outs.ts`. Add a 10% constant. Move the E:1/E:2 block out of the percentage calculator into `license-determination` Gate 1.5 (screening flag, not flat block). Repoint tier helpers to `resolveCountryGroups`. Re-export `DeMinimisResult` type from a `subject-to-ear` module.
- **Files:** `de-minimis-calculator.ts`, `country-groups.ts`, `classify-item.ts`, `license-determination.ts`, `bom-de-minimis/calculator.ts` (DM-3 hint rename), the two ClassificationPanel consumers.
- **Tests:** golden-master of `computeClassification`; cross-engine consistency; RED tests (15% to Iran → 10%-eligible not 0%-block; 600-series .y to PRC → 0%; D:1 China 20% → 25%); a structural test forbidding embargo/D:1 literals containing IR/KP outside `country-groups.ts`.
- **AWV rename (separate commit, no logic change):** `art9`→`para8` in `catch-all-evaluator.ts` + `recompute.server.ts:137`; additive `catchAllPara8Hit Boolean @default(false)` at `schema.prisma:11742`, backfill from `catchAllArt9Hit`, dual-write, drop old column in a follow-up migration; update UI (`BafaElanK2Document.tsx`, both operations pages) + tests.

### P0-C — NOAA CRSRA remote-sensing engine: WIRE (not remove) (NOAA-WIRE-1) · MEDIUM

**Decision.** The regime (51 U.S.C. 60121 + 15 CFR Part 960, Tier 1/2/3) is **real and faithfully modeled** but has zero non-test consumers. Recommend **WIRE**, not delete. Add `remote-sensing.server.ts` loading a `RemoteSensingOperatorProfile`; add org-scoped, rate-limited `GET/POST /api/trade/remote-sensing/route.ts`; register an Astra tool `assess_remote_sensing_compliance`; surface on a counterparty/operator tab. **Before shipping, fix two dataset citation-staleness items:** source URL points to subchapter-C but Part 960 is now subchapter-D; `NOAA-960-6-TIER` title should be "Application categorization" (official heading), not the paraphrase. Primary: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-15/subtitle-B/chapter-IX/subchapter-D/part-960/subpart-B/section-960.6

### P0-D — Orbis UBO honest gating + snapshot-staleness gate · **HIGH + CRITICAL · challenges CONFIRMED**

- **ORBIS-UBO-MOCK-2 (HIGH).** `getOrbisUboAdapter()` unconditionally returns the fixture mock; no real adapter exists. It feeds the cascade only via the **read-only** `/ubo` route (not the persisted `screenParty→runCascadeForParty` path, which ignores UBO entirely). Risk: fixture-keyed test party → fabricated cascade hit; every real party → silent `resolved:false` (false assurance). **Fix:** branch on `ORBIS_API_KEY`/`UBO_SOURCE` env — when unset return a typed `UboSourceNotConfiguredError` adapter (NOT the mock; mock only under `NODE_ENV==='test'`/`UBO_USE_FIXTURES`); add `source:'live'|'mock'|'unconfigured'` to the summary + UI chip; add a `minUboConfidence` floor (default 0.5) before splicing UBO edges; **decide deliberately** whether UBO should affect the persisted verdict (today it does not — a silent gap). Real-integration alternative: `orbis-ubo-real.ts` (BvD/Moody's client) behind the same contract. Primary: https://ofac.treasury.gov/faqs/401
- **SNAPSHOT-STALENESS-3 (CRITICAL).** No TTL/age check on `TradeSanctionsSnapshot`; `screenParty` only escalates on **missing** critical lists (T-H3), never **stale** ones → a party can be CLEAR against a months-old OFAC list (cron silently broken). **Fix:** add a per-list `MAX_SNAPSHOT_AGE` policy (OFAC_SDN/EU_FSF/UN/BIS = 48h; supplementary = 7d) via `getEffectiveScreeningConfig`; compute `staleCritical` and fold into the existing escalation (CLEAR→POTENTIAL_MATCH with a **distinct** rationale); add `staleLists` to the result + `logger.warn` + an ops alert; surface `fetchedAt`/age per list in the UI; extend `screen-party.server.test.ts`. Primary: https://ofac.treasury.gov/faqs/20 (no SLA — the TTL is a fail-closed policy choice, not a regulatory constant).

> **Surface/branch note (all P0 items):** This is the Trade (Passage) surface. Per the standing surface-separation guard, these belong on a **Trade branch (`fix/trade-to-92`)** under the Trade MASTER plan (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-30-caelex-trade-to-92-MASTER.md`), **not** the `fix-atlas-correctness` branch.

---

## 6. Recommended Execution Order

**Wave 1 — CRITICAL data fixes that are pure deletions/value-edits (apply immediately; lowest risk, highest payoff):**

1. **CG-UA-D1** — remove `"UA"` from D:1 (auto-fixes D:3/D:4). *(Note: the broader D:1 *repopulation* is blocked on lawyer review — see Wave 4.)*
2. **TH-1** — flip the XV(a)(7)(i) aperture operator to `gt` (genuine leak). Confirm 0.50 m boundary vs EAR `between`.
3. **CITE-3 + CITE-4** — replace the fabricated OFAC citation and GVA fact-pattern across all files (liability-grade; no logic change).
4. **CG-A5-MALTA-WRONG / CG-A6-MISSING / CG-A5-MISSING-IN-KR** — A:5/A:6 membership edits (HIGH, no challenge).
5. **MTCR-9A101/9A106/9A108/9A110** — remove the 4 Cat-II ECCNs from the `risk-score.ts` Cat-I array (then do MTCR-RISKSCORE-LIST as the structural follow-up). **Re-cite to live MTCR Annex/eCFR before commit.**

**Wave 2 — CRITICAL source/coverage repoints (operational; verify redirect-following):** 6. **SRC-UK-1** (+ SRC-UK-2) — repoint to FCDO UKSL with a new parser branch. **UK screening matches nobody today — top operational priority.** 7. **SRC-ANNEXIV-1** — wire the consolidated-ELI Annex IV sync; add a partial-coverage warning until loaded. 8. **SRC-OFAC-1/2, SRC-EUFSF-1, SRC-UN-1** — repoint to canonical URLs; verify the HTTP client follows 302/307; wire OFAC alias merge.

**Wave 3 — CRITICAL engineering (designs ready; TDD, Trade branch):** 9. **P0-A (DCW-1)** — corpus wiring (closes the declared-code leak). 10. **P0-B (DM-1/DM-3)** — de-minimis single-source + AWV rename. 11. **P0-D — SNAPSHOT-STALENESS-3** then **ORBIS-UBO-MOCK-2** (staleness is CRITICAL; UBO gating HIGH).

**Wave 4 — remaining CONFIRMED edits + caveat re-cites:** 12. **TH-6, TH-9** (parametric); **FDPR-5, FDPR-6** (FDPR text); **DM-4/DM-5/DM-6** (de-minimis labels); **AWV-2/AWV-4/AWV-5** (German/EU citation labels). Each is CONFIRMED; the MTCR/`CG-IN-D3`/`CG-D3-D4`/`FDPR-6` ones need the human re-cite noted in §3. 13. **P0-C (NOAA-WIRE-1)** — wire the remote-sensing engine.

**Blocked on lawyer sign-off (do NOT auto-apply — see §3):**

- **CG-D1-INCOMPLETE + FDPR-7** — the full D:1 set must be repopulated from the **48-country eCFR D:1 column**, NOT the agent's refuted 24-country list. The UA _removal_ alone (Wave 1 #1) is safe; the D:1 _completion_ is the blocked part and is itself a potential CRITICAL under-coverage (missing Iran/Syria) if done from the wrong list.
- **AWV-3** — the §9(2)-as-arms-embargo-catch-all is fabricated; re-basing onto §74 ff. AWV / EU-CFSP embargoes and auditing the 19-country list (IQ embargo lifted, CU is OFAC, CN contested) requires a German export-control lawyer.
- **AWV-6** — the `WMD_CONCERN_COUNTRIES` overlay (IL/IN) has no primary source; a lawyer must approve relabeling it as heuristic-only.
- **CG-D3-D4-CONSTRUCTION** — fix direction is confirmed (apply the structural change) but the exact D:3/D:4 member lists must be transcribed column-by-column from the GPO PDF and verified by a human; do not paste the agent's unverified enumerations.

**Relevant files (absolute paths):**

- `/Users/julianpolleschner/caelex-assessment/src/lib/trade/subject-to-ear/country-groups.ts`
- `/Users/julianpolleschner/caelex-assessment/src/lib/trade/subject-to-ear/fdpr-engine.ts`
